_doc: Asset-tagging configuration for SecurityInsight. Copy this file to 'asset-tagging.config.json' in the same directory (gitignored) and flip the booleans you want. The tagging engine reads the JSON at startup and gates every rule whose YAML has a matching 'EnabledByConfigFlag:' field; rules without that field always fire regardless of this file.

AutoExcludeRules._doc: Auto-exclusion rules: each flag, when true, makes the tagging engine emit an MDE machine tag of '<bucket>--Excluded--SI' on every device whose Defender XDR OnboardingStatus matches the bucket. The existing Risk Analysis + asset-profiling exclusion machinery (RiskAnalysisGlobalExclusions.custom.json's ExcludedAssetTags: ['--Excluded--SI']) then drops those devices from every RA report and from every profiling rule automatically. Flip these on selectively -- excluding 'CanBeOnboarded' devices means you accept they're invisible to MDE / SI until you onboard them, which is normally fine for short-lived test machines but NOT for production endpoints in flight.
AutoExcludeRules._ExcludeCanBeOnboardedDoc: When true, tag every device with Defender XDR DeviceInfo.OnboardingStatus = 'Can be onboarded' (Defender sees the device but Sense agent isn't installed yet). Use when you want to suppress noise from devices in the middle of an onboarding rollout.
AutoExcludeRules._ExcludeInsufficientInfoDoc: When true, tag every device with OnboardingStatus = 'Insufficient info' (Defender has signal traces but not enough to identify the device class). Use when transient appliances / IoT scanners keep flagging.
AutoExcludeRules._ExcludeUnsupportedDoc: When true, tag every device with OnboardingStatus = 'Unsupported' (legacy / appliance OS that Defender can't onboard at all). Use when retired EOL devices are still detectable in EG but you've accepted that risk.
